articles.title: Sonam Wangchuk removed from Jantar Mantar on Day 21 of hunger strike, taken to hospital; chaos erupts
articles.description: In a video message shared at the end of the Day 20 of his fast on Friday, Wangchuk urged people to join the CJP Parliament march on July 20 in large numbers, saying public participation was the movement's biggest strength. "Yes, I am still alive. Twenty per cent of my body is gone. After fats, muscles are gone. After that, organs will go. Finally, the brain. The time has not come yet," Wangchuk said.

articles.title: After Andhra, Covid-19 resurfaces in Maharashtra after 3 years
articles.description: Maharashtra has seen a rise in Covid-19 cases, mostly mild, over recent days.  Experts note the virus now follows influenza-like patterns with periodic spikes.  Most positive cases were detected during physician-advised investigations or pre-operative assessments.  This recent uptick is the first noticeable increase in Covid cases since around 2023.  Hospitals are also observing increased cases of influenza and other infections.
articles.full_text: Neha Madaan is a senior feature writer at The Times of India, Pune. She holds an M A degree in Mass Communication and Journalism from University of Pune. She covers tourism, heritage development and its conservation, apart from an array of subjects such as civic issues, environment, astronomy, civic school education as well as social issues concerning persons with disabilities. articles.title: Lamine Yamal separated from Spain squad ahead of final against Argentina
articles.description: Spain have eased concerns over Lamine Yamal's fitness after the teenager trained separately ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup final against Argentina. The RFEF and coach Luis de la Fuente insist the session was precautionary, with Pedro Porro also on an individual programme. Both players are expected to be fit as Spain chase a fourth World Cup title on Sunday.

articles.title: Who is Tiana Musarra? Everything to know about the influencer making headlines at Streamer University 2026
articles.description: Tiana Musarra is a lifestyle influencer, model and Twitch streamer who gained wider recognition after joining Kai Cenat’s Streamer University 2026. She previously built an audience through fashion, fitness and GRWM content and has over 123,000 Twitch followers. Her name recently drew extra attention after Josh “YourRAGE” mentioned her during a heated on-stream call with DDG.

articles.title: Burnham is Labour chief, to take over as PM on July 20
Mukbang YouTuber Yuno apologizes after plane food ordering video sparks backlash
articles.description: Mukbang creator Yuno apologized after backlash for a long-haul flight video. He filmed himself ordering food more than twenty times during the fifteen-hour journey. Viewers criticized Yuno for creating extra work for flight attendants and disturbing passengers. The YouTuber deleted the video and admitted his decision was poor and inconsiderate. This incident sparked discussions about influencer responsibility in public spaces.

articles.title: Textile sector to sew loose ends as FTAs kick in
articles.description: New free trade agreements offer Indian textile exporters significant tariff advantages. The UK deal eliminates up to twelve percent tariffs, boosting competitiveness against rivals. Companies anticipate increased orders and potential export growth over the next few years. However, fragmented supply chains and higher input costs present ongoing challenges. Industry experts suggest government incentives could strengthen domestic fabric ecosystems.

articles.title: ODI tickets worth Rs 52.36 lakh went unsold: Khanna
articles.description: "Tickets worth Rupees 52,36,000 were unsold. Why all the tickets were not sold-out? DDCA started online ticket sale quite late that too without proper advertisement," Khanna claimed in a media release.

articles.title: She pursued MBBS and IIT Madras at the same time, today, she's using AI to advance cancer research
articles.description: BVS Madhavi pursued an unconventional path by combining an MBBS degree from Andhra Medical College with a BS in Data Science and Programming from IIT Madras. Her interdisciplinary journey led her to work on AI-driven healthcare solutions, including medical imaging and oncology research. As a Junior Clinical Scientist at nference, she applies artificial intelligence to generate real-world evidence for cancer research. Her story highlights how medicine and technology can converge to shape the future of healthcare.

articles.title: NEET UG 2026 cutoff rises sharply, students fear government medical seats may slip away despite 600 marks
articles.description: The rise in NEET-UG qualifying cutoff marks has created uncertainty among medical aspirants preparing for admissions this year. Students are concerned that even scores between 500 and 600 marks may not guarantee government medical college seats due to increased competition. The article examines the category-wise cutoff jump, student concerns, counselling queries received by CBSE experts and expert views on how higher scores could impact admission requirements.

articles.title: Rickshaw puller's grandson becomes first from Bihar village to crack NEET UG, advises consistency to aspirants
articles.description: Aditya from Ratnauli village in Muzaffarpur has emerged as a symbol of determination after securing AIR 946 in NEET UG, becoming the first student from his village to crack the medical entrance exam. His grandfather Saukhi Sahni was a former rickshaw puller, while his father works in a private job. The article highlights Aditya’s achievement along with other Muzaffarpur students who secured notable NEET-UG ranks through dedicated preparation.

articles.title: Meet the 45-year-old mother and her 21-year-old son who graduated from IIT Madras together
articles.description: Jigisha Tailor and her son Aditya Kapadia earned degrees together at IIT Madras. Aditya enrolled in the online BS program in 2021, which his mother joined later. Jigisha balanced her studies with household chores and a decade-long academic break. They became study partners and academic competitors, aiming for the highest distinction. Their shared journey brought the mother and son closer, adding a new dimension to their relationship.

articles.title: Tiny Tom responds to Salim Kumar's funeral trolling
articles.description: Actor Tiny Tom responded to the backlash concerning his tearful farewell at Salim Kumar's funeral. He reminisced about their nearly three-decade friendship that blossomed through mimicry and competing in college events. Tiny Tom fondly shared anecdotes of their camaraderie and victories at university functions. He had visited Salim Kumar in the hospital, praying for a miracle, making his loss feel even more profound.

articles.title: 'Welcome 3' earns just Rs 20 lakh amid 'The Odyssey' wave
articles.description: Welcome To The Jungle's box office earnings have seen a sharp decline in its fourth week. The film earned Rs 20 lakh on Friday, a 60 percent drop from Thursday. Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' emerged as a strong competitor, impacting collections significantly. Worldwide gross collections for 'Welcome To The Jungle' are nearing Rs 200 crore. Further declines are expected with the upcoming release of 'Spider-Man: Brand New Day'.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Arnold Schwarzenegger
articles.description: Arnold Schwarzenegger's life story highlights dedication and unwavering belief in achieving dreams. He transitioned from bodybuilding champion to a successful Hollywood actor and politician. Schwarzenegger's career demonstrates that consistent effort transforms visions into tangible realities. His journey underscores the importance of action alongside ambition for success. The article reflects on his inspirational autobiography, 'Total Recall'.

articles.title: Deepak Parambol recalls 'Varavu' shoot accident
articles.description: Actor Deepak Parambol recently shared details about a dangerous accident during the filming of 'Varavu'. He was hanging from a moving jeep when the vehicle overturned while taking a turn. Deepak realized he survived purely by luck and instinct saved him. He sustained only minor cuts on his knee and hand from the incident. The action drama directed by Shaji Kailas was released on July 16.
